concerns.text: FR-007 and US-3 acceptance criteria explicitly mandate 'paired t-tests' to compare prediction errors across density bins. Tasks T027 and T028 implement 'One-way ANOVA' and 'Tukey HSD' instead. While the plan.md notes this as a 'Statistical Analysis Correction', the tasks document silently relaxes the functional requirement defined in the spec without a corresponding amendment to the spec or an explicit 'staged simplification' note in the task description. This is a semantic weakening of the FR.

SC-002 requires measuring the 'degradation rate of accuracy for out-of-distribution densities'. Tasks T029 and T031 only cover 'flagging instances' and 'detecting failures'. There is no task explicitly defined to calculate and report the quantitative 'degradation rate' metric required to verify SC-002. The acceptance criterion is dropped from the task verification steps.

Constitution Principle VI mandates 'analytical homogenization formulas'. The plan.md and tasks.md (T004) implement 'FFT-based numerical homogenization'. While the plan notes this as a necessary amendment for scientific validity, the tasks document proceeds with the FFT implementation without a visible 'Constitution Amendment' marker or a specific task to formally amend the constitution file (FR-030) before execution. This introduces a silent constitution drift where the implementation violates the current legal governance document.
